module.title: SAP TM Organizational Structure & Master Data

topic.title: SAP TM Organizational Assignment & Planning Profiles

learningObjectives.title: Understand organizational assignment
learningObjectives.description: Explain how transportation documents are mapped to planning organizations and planners.

learningObjectives.title: Understand planning profiles
learningObjectives.description: Identify profile components that control planning behavior and optimization.

learningObjectives.title: Differentiate assignment and profile
learningObjectives.description: Clarify responsibility assignment versus planning strategy configuration.

sections.content: Organizational Assignment in SAP TM connects transportation business documents with the appropriate planning organization, planning profile and planner. It ensures that every transportation request is handled by the correct business unit according to organizational rules.

sections.title: Key Concept
sections.content: Planning Profiles define HOW transportation planning is performed, while Organizational Assignment defines WHO performs the planning.
A Planning Profile contains planning parameters, optimization settings, default values and planning strategies used by transportation planners while creating Freight Orders.

Transportation Cockpit is the primary planning workspace in SAP TM where planners manage Freight Units, optimize transportation, create Freight Orders and monitor planning activities using Planning Profiles.

ABC Logistics has different planning teams for Domestic and International transportation. Domestic planners use a Planning Profile optimized for road transportation, while International planners use a profile supporting ocean and air freight planning.

sections.title: Real Project Example
sections.content: A retail company configured separate Planning Profiles for Road, Rail and Ocean transportation. Planners automatically received the correct profile based on transportation mode, improving planning efficiency.

sections.title: Interview Tip
sections.content: Expect interview questions on Transportation Cockpit and Planning Profiles. Be able to explain how planners use them during Freight Order creation and optimization.

sections.title: Common Mistake
sections.content: Planning Profile is not the same as Planning Organization. Planning Organization identifies the responsible business unit, whereas Planning Profile controls the planning behavior.

items: Transportation Cockpit is the planner's workspace.
Different Planning Profiles can be configured for different transportation scenarios.
